summary: Innovation Fund Denmark has granted 17.4 million Danish kroner to the ODAQS project, led by Professor Jaco van de Pol at Aarhus University, in collaboration with Aalborg University and quantum technology company Kvantify. Running from 2026 to 2029, ODAQS aims to develop a software stack that simplifies and optimizes quantum computer use in drug discovery, addressing current barriers to practical application. The project will deploy reinforcement learning to automate and improve the efficiency of quantum software, targeting reduced time and costs in pharmaceutical research. According to Allan Grønlund, Kvantify's co-founder and Chief Technology Officer, ODAQS provides “an important technological foundation for bringing quantum computing closer to industrial application.”
